# Business Rules — Terry's Vinyl

Invariants that must hold at all times. Any change that violates a rule requires an explicit decision and an update to this document.

---

## Collection Records

**BR-001** — Every record must have a non-empty `artist` and `album`. These are the only required fields.

**BR-002** — Every record has a unique `id` generated by `crypto.randomUUID()`. IDs are immutable once assigned.

**BR-003** — `addedAt` is set at creation time and never mutated on edit.

**BR-004** — Optional fields (`year`, `genre`, `notes`) may be empty strings. They are never `null` or `undefined` in storage.

---

## Storage

**BR-005** — `data.json` is the single source of truth. Client state must always be derived from a successful server read.

**BR-006** — Every write to `data.json` must validate the payload as parseable JSON before writing. Invalid JSON must be rejected with a 400 response.

**BR-007** — The server writes the entire collection array on every save (full replace, not patch). This is acceptable given the expected collection size.

---

## API

**BR-008** — `GET /api/collection` returns the full collection array. It never returns a subset.

**BR-009** — `POST /api/collection` replaces the entire collection. The client is responsible for sending the complete updated array.

**BR-010** — No authentication is required. The server is local-only (binds to `localhost:3000`).

---

## UI

**BR-011** — Disc art colours are deterministic: the same artist + album always produces the same colour palette. This must not be randomised at render time.

**BR-012** — Genre filter options are derived exclusively from genres present in the current collection. No hardcoded genre list.
